sourceSections: The Ornith 1.0 Release and Initial Misconception
Deep Dive into Ornith's Architecture and Performance
Understanding Claude Code as an Agent Harness
The Synergy: Brain and Hands Working Together

sections.lead: Ornith 1.0, released June 25, 2026, by Deep Reinforce, was an open-weight coding model initially mistaken for an agent harness competitor, clarified as a "brain."

bullets.label: Release Details
bullets.text: Deep Reinforce released Ornith 1.0 on June 25th, 2026, as an open-weight, MIT-licensed coding model.

bullets.label: Core Feature
bullets.text: Its significant feature is autonomously learning and generating its own scaffold, plan, and tool calls, plus checking and repairing its work.

bullets.label: Initial Misconception
bullets.text: Many assumed Ornith would compete with or replace agent harnesses such as Claude Code.

bullets.label: Role Clarification
bullets.text: The video clarifies Ornith is a "brain" and Claude Code is "hands," meaning they are different tools designed to compose.

bullets.label: Team Use
bullets.text: Ornith's development team even used Claude Code as a harness to measure its performance.

sections.lead: Deep Reinforce's Ornith models, built on Gemma 4 and Quinn 3.5, come in four sizes with large context windows and frontier-scale performance.

bullets.label: Model Basis
bullets.text: Ornith is a family of open coding models from Deep Reinforce, built upon Gemma 4 and Quinn 3.5, enhanced with reinforcement learning.

bullets.label: Four Sizes
bullets.text: It comes in four sizes: 9 billion, 31 billion (dense workhorse), 35 billion (mixture of experts), and a 397 billion flagship model.

bullets.label: Context Windows
bullets.text: All versions support large context windows ranging from 250,000 to 400,000 tokens.

bullets.label: Agentic Features
bullets.text: The models are agentic, emitting visible thinking blocks and clean, OpenAI-style tool calls.

bullets.label: Flagship Performance
bullets.text: The 397 billion flagship model achieves 82.4 on SWE bench verified, matching Claude Opus 4.7 and closely trailing 4.8.

sections.lead: Ornith's self-scaffolding process allows it to learn "how to work" efficiently over thousands of rounds, outperforming larger competitors like Quinn 3.5.

bullets.label: Unique Learning
bullets.text: Ornith learns to design, refine, and rewrite its own operational process, unlike models with fixed human-written scaffolds.

bullets.label: Two-Stage Process
bullets.text: Its two-stage process involves proposing a scaffold, using it to solve a task, and feeding performance rewards back into planning and execution.

bullets.label: Guardrails Present
bullets.text: Guardrails like a trust boundary, a deterministic monitor, and a frozen judge model prevent gaming the system.

bullets.label: Efficiency Gain
bullets.text: The 9-billion parameter model efficiently outperforms larger competitors, including Quinn 3.5 and Gemma 4, on SWE bench.

sections.lead: Claude Code is an agent harness, not an LLM, that orchestrates real-world actions, manages environment interactions, and can drive different models.

bullets.label: Not LLM
bullets.text: Claude Code is an agent harness, not a large language model, that orchestrates a model's actions.

bullets.label: Real-World Actions
bullets.text: It translates a model's generated text into real-world actions such as opening files, running commands, and handling errors.

bullets.label: Environmental Control
bullets.text: The harness manages critical elements like file system access, terminal interaction, and external tool connections, ensuring safety.

bullets.label: Brain Agnostic
bullets.text: Claude Code is brain-agnostic and can drive different models by simply changing an environment variable.

sections.lead: Combining Ornith's "brain" (planning) with Claude Code's "hands" (execution) creates a powerful, safe synergy, rather than competition.

bullets.label: Category Error
bullets.text: The idea of Ornith (brain) replacing Claude Code (hands) is a "category error" because their functions differ.

bullets.label: Ornith's Focus
bullets.text: Ornith's internal scaffolding focuses on planning and tool selection within its cognitive process.

bullets.label: Claude Code's Role
bullets.text: Claude Code provides the essential runtime scaffolding and environmental control for safe execution.

bullets.label: Powerful Synergy
bullets.text: Combining Ornith's planning capabilities with Claude Code's safe execution environment creates a powerful synergy.

bullets.label: Safe Execution
bullets.text: This setup enables a model that knows how to act, safely performing actions on your machine with guardrails and user approval.

sections.lead: Integrating Ornith with Claude Code involves serving Ornith locally, using a translator for communication, and configuring Claude Code to point to this setup.

bullets.label: Serve Ornith
bullets.text: Serve the Ornith model locally using a tool like VLLM, setting it up as an OpenAI-compatible endpoint.

bullets.label: Add Translator
bullets.text: Introduce a small translator or proxy to bridge communication between Claude Code's Anthropic message format and Ornith's OpenAI style.

bullets.label: Configure Claude Code
bullets.text: Configure Claude Code by setting the base URL variable to point to the proxy and specifying Ornith as the model.

bullets.label: Local Execution
bullets.text: This setup allows Claude Code to leverage Ornith as its underlying "brain" for local, agentic code execution with user approval.

sections.lead: Ornith offers a powerful open "brain" for Claude Code, ideal for local, cost-sensitive tasks, significantly narrowing the gap with frontier models.

bullets.label: Flagship Performance
bullets.text: Ornith's flagship model performs remarkably well, matching Claude Opus 4.7 and slightly trailing Opus 4.8 on SWE bench.

bullets.label: Frontier Advantage
bullets.text: Frontier closed models still hold an edge in the hardest, longest-horizon tasks.

bullets.label: Ornith's Niche
bullets.text: Ornith excels when code must remain local, offline, under a permissive license, or when avoiding per-token costs is crucial.

bullets.label: Key Strategy
bullets.text: The key insight is to use Claude Code as a constant harness and swap the "brain" (Ornith or a frontier model) to fit the job.

bullets.label: Gap Narrows
bullets.text: This development signifies a significant narrowing of the gap between open-weight and frontier AI models.
